rationale: CS & AI is 11% of all papers, second only to Medicine. The community has stable, sharp subdisciplines and people identify strongly with them ('I'm an NLP person', 'I'm a systems person'). I anchored on arXiv's cs.* categories as the de facto identity map for the field but collapsed where the picker should — e.g. arXiv has cs.LG, cs.AI, cs.CL, cs.CV, cs.RO as separate, but a level-2 picker doesn't need that granularity (all are level-3 children of AI/ML). I split Theory of Computation from Algorithms because they're separate identities (a TOC researcher proves things, an algorithms researcher engineers things). I kept Robotics here even though Electrical Engineering also has a robotics-EE — the CS-flavored robotics (perception, planning, learning) lives here; the hardware-flavored robotics (actuation, control) lives in EE. I added AI Safety/Alignment as its own card because it's exploded in identity over the past 3 years and most papers self-identify there rather than into ML.

subspecialties.name: Machine Learning
covers: supervised/unsupervised/RL methods, optimization theory, deep learning architecture, ML theory, scaling laws
NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR core ML papers, arXiv cs.LG

subspecialties.name: Natural Language Processing
covers: LLM training and evaluation, dialogue systems, machine translation, semantic parsing, low-resource NLP, multilingual models, RAG
ACL, EMNLP, NAACL, arXiv cs.CL
explicitly_excludes: speech recognition acoustic modeling → Electrical Engineering

subspecialties.name: Computer Vision
covers: image classification/detection/segmentation, generative vision (diffusion, GANs), video understanding, 3D vision, neural rendering, medical imaging methods
CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, arXiv cs.CV
explicitly_excludes: clinical validation of imaging models → Medicine

subspecialties.name: Robotics
covers: robot learning, manipulation, locomotion, SLAM, motion planning, multi-robot systems, embodied AI
ICRA, IROS, RSS, CoRL, arXiv cs.RO
explicitly_excludes: robot hardware / actuators → Electrical Engineering or Mechanical Engineering

subspecialties.name: AI Safety & Alignment
covers: alignment research, mechanistic interpretability, evals, RLHF and beyond, AI governance, safety benchmarks, model welfare

subspecialties.name: Systems
subspecialties.tagline: Operating systems, distributed systems, datacenter, networks.
covers: OS, distributed systems, networking, storage, datacenter computing, edge computing, virtualization, performance engineering
OSDI, SOSP, NSDI, ATC, arXiv cs.DC, cs.NI, cs.OS
explicitly_excludes: network theory / graph algorithms → Theory of Computation

subspecialties.name: Security & Cryptography
subspecialties.tagline: Crypto, network security, privacy, formal methods for security.
covers: applied cryptography, post-quantum crypto, network and systems security, privacy (DP, HE), web security, side channels, blockchain protocols
S&P, USENIX Sec, CCS, NDSS, CRYPTO

subspecialties.name: Theory of Computation
covers: complexity theory, computational learning theory, algorithm design and analysis, computational geometry, combinatorial optimization theory
STOC, FOCS, SODA, ICALP

subspecialties.name: Programming Languages & Software Engineering
covers: PL design and theory, type systems, compilers, formal verification, software engineering methodology, software testing
POPL, PLDI, ICSE, FSE, OOPSLA
explicitly_excludes: model-checking applied to security → Security & Cryptography

subspecialties.name: Databases & Information Retrieval
subspecialties.tagline: DB systems, query optimization, search, vector indexes.
covers: database systems, query processing, transaction systems, information retrieval, search engines, vector databases, knowledge graphs
SIGMOD, VLDB, ICDE, SIGIR
explicitly_excludes: semantic search method via LLMs → NLP

subspecialties.name: Human-Computer Interaction
subspecialties.tagline: Interaction design, accessibility, social computing, AR/VR.
covers: interaction design, accessibility, social computing, AR/VR/XR, CSCW, ubiquitous computing, HCI of AI
CHI, UIST, CSCW
explicitly_excludes: psychology methods of interaction → Brain & Cognitive Sciences

subspecialties.name: Computational Science
subspecialties.tagline: Numerical methods, scientific computing, simulation, HPC.
covers: numerical analysis, PDE solvers, scientific computing, high-performance computing, simulation methods, computational physics methods
SISC, JCP, SC conference
explicitly_excludes: applied science from a single domain → that domain

subspecialties.name: AI for Science
subspecialties.tagline: ML applied to chemistry, biology, physics, materials.
covers: ML for chemistry, materials, biology, physics; generative models for science; neural ODEs for science; AI4Sci foundation models
Nat Mach Intell AI4Sci papers

subspecialties.name: Computer Architecture & Hardware
subspecialties.tagline: Chip design, ML accelerators, memory, hardware-software co-design.
covers: computer architecture, GPU/TPU/accelerator design, memory systems, hardware-software co-design, energy-efficient computing
ISCA, MICRO, HPCA, ASPLOS

subspecialties.name: Graphics & Visualization
subspecialties.tagline: Rendering, simulation, geometry, visualization for data.
covers: rendering, geometry processing, animation, physics-based simulation for graphics, data visualization, visual analytics
SIGGRAPH, IEEE Vis, EuroVis
explicitly_excludes: neural rendering as vision → Computer Vision

subspecialties.name: Computing & Society
subspecialties.tagline: FAccT, computing ethics, digital labor, policy, education.
covers: fairness, accountability, transparency (FAccT), algorithmic bias, computing ethics, computer science education, digital labor studies
explicitly_excludes: AI safety technical research → AI Safety & Alignment

uncovered_concerns: Considered separating Reinforcement Learning from ML — RL is huge and identity-distinct, but it'd hollow ML; left as RL-under-ML for the picker, level 3 can split.

Considered Quantum Computing — most of it lives in Physics & Astronomy (quantum info as physics) but quantum algorithms / quantum complexity is real CS. Folded under Theory of Computation as quantum algorithms; pure quantum hardware → Physics.
Considered Multi-Agent Systems — too small at level 2, folds under ML or Robotics.
Considered Recommender Systems / Ads ML — landed under ML; identity is weaker than the other splits.
